steps.title: Support both parts of Hale
steps.content: Hale argues that the pieces likely came from a minstrel's repertoire book and from a minstrel working near Avery's home. Strong support should connect the texts to performance and to that local area. The quoted features already suggest live performance, so the strongest new evidence should also explain why the repertoire would belong to someone performing near Avery rather than somewhere else.

steps.title: Choose the local evidence
steps.content: A concerns manuscript history, not performance or place. B discusses audience type and Avery's broader interests without tying the pieces locally. C weakens Hale by showing repetition can appear in nonperformed texts. Local references familiar to Avery's nearby audience support Hale. The answer is D.
